chunk:
CHUNK:020/021
But neither did I care as my fingers grasped the corner of the diary, and I clutched it to my chest as the cold cramped my fingers and I stroked, one handed, back to the sinking boat. \u201cYou are certifiable, girl,\u201d Rachael said as she reached for me. \u201cYes,\u201d I said, teeth chattering as she pulled me over the flat tube and onto the metal deck. \u201cWhat the hell is so important about that book, anyway?\u201d she said. \u201cI don\u2019t know.\u201d I shivered for a few seconds, trying to speak. And then, as I saw Gaba in a Zodiac racing towards me, I looked at Rachael and said, \u201cI haven\u2019t read it yet.\u201d \n 15 It took six duvets, four hours, and one electric blanket before I felt warm again. Gaba sat in the armchair at the end of the bed, removing grit from my MP5 with a cleaning kit he said he liberated from the Americans. He looked up from time to time, and every time I stirred, he looked at me. I caught his eye, but with my hands curled around the corner of the duvet, I kept my mouth and nose covered, hiding my smile as he watched over me. I would never tell Atii, but not doing something naughty with Gaba, was almost more exciting that doing what I sometimes fantasized about doing with the big, bald, macho leader of Greenland\u2019s SRU. I know. Hopeless. But then, wasn\u2019t it okay to be a hopeless romantic once in a while? \u201cI know you\u2019re awake, Petra,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cMy eyes are open.\u201d \u201cAap. But you\u2019ve been awake for about two hours. Am I right?\u201d I said nothing, preferring not to reveal that I had secretly watched him for almost as long as he had been watching me. He turned his head and nodded at the windowsill, and the bundle of towels within which I hoped I would find Paniula\u2019s diary. \u201cIt\u2019s ruined, of course,\u201d he said, and I sighed. \u201cThe ink has run on most of the pages, but the deeper you get into the book\u2026\u201d \u201cYes?\u201d I said, not daring to hope too much. \u201cWell, it\u2019s possible\u2026\u201d \u201cYes?\u201d \u201cI said it\u2019s possible, Petra, that you might be able to read some of it. When it\u2019s dried out, of course.\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. \u201cBut before then, you\u2019ll want to know about Rachael and the rest of her merry band of diamond smugglers.\u201d \u201cHer band?\u201d \u201cAap,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cThat\u2019s what Barry says.\u201d Gaba raised a thick finger to stop me from talking as he continued. \u201cThe three guys were what Barry called pastries, I think.\u201d I giggled, and then pulled the covers down to say, \u201cPatsies. Fall guys. Or dupes.\u201d \u201cWell, whatever they are, he said Rachael picked them up, and paid them a tonne of cash to pretend they were Sean Arnet and Co. She even paid them to stick the diamond jewellery in passengers\u2019 pockets.\u201d I was still processing the thought that there were three patsies, when I realised Gaba had moved on. \u201c\u2026confiscated as evidence, of course,\u201d he said. \u201cWhat was?\u201d \u201cThe jewellery,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cBarry took it all as evidence, and he\u2019s taking it all back to the States, along with Rachael and the three guys.\u201d \u201cIt wasn\u2019t fake, then?\u201d \u201cThe jewellery?\u201d Gaba shrugged. \u201cWho knows, and who cares? We\u2019re done with this one, Constable.\u201d Gaba slid the MP5 and the cleaning kit onto the table and stood up. \u201cFunny thing about Barry\u2026\u201d \u201cYes?\u201d \u201cHe was a last-minute replacement. Apparently, the other guy who was supposed to lead the operation was sick with something. Barry just happened to be in Greenland, and stepped up, as they say.\u201d \u201cWhat was Barry\u2019s last name?\u201d Gaba shook his head and said, \u201cI can\u2019t remember. He probably told me. But the thing about him was there was nothing memorable about him. He had brown hair \u2013 a little thin, maybe. He wore boring clothes. Nothing stood out. Not even his accent. Everything about Barry was\u2026\u201d \u201cBland,\u201d I said, cutting Gaba off. \u201cAap. That\u2019s what I was going to say. \u201cHis name is Barry August,\u201d I said, remembering what Rachael had told me in the longhouse. \u201cHe\u2019s\u2026\u201d I sat up, suddenly warm and awake, only to grab the duvets again as I discovered I was naked. \u201cRight,\u201d Gaba said. \u201cAbout that. You were cold, Petra. You were shivering. I had to get your wet clothes off you, and\u2026\u201d I gave Gaba a hard stare and then said, \u201cAnd what about Saarullik and Taateraaq?\u201d Gaba shook his head. \u201cOn the way back to Nuuk. We\u2019re on the next flight, but you can take the ship a little further up the coast if you\u2026\u201d \u201cYes?\u201d I said. Gaba held up his finger again, and I waited for him to catch on to what I said earlier. It was like watching the dawn break in the far north \u2013 a pale light growing stronger and sharper by the second. \u201cWhat did you say the guy\u2019s name was?\u201d \u201cBarry August.\u201d \u201cAugust as in\u2026\u201d \u201cSean Augustus Arnet,\u201d I said, suddenly convinced that it was all starting to make sense. \u201cThe man who flew in as a replacement at the last minute and confiscated all the fake diamonds.\u201d \u201cAap\u2026 And\u2026\u201d \u201cDiamonds we think are fake.\u201d \u201cSure, but all those men with guns, Petra. They were the real deal.\u201d \u201cThe real deal who probably just made a hefty bonus on the side on an operation in a country most people have never thought about, or even heard of.\u201d \u201cSo you think\u2026\u201d \u201cYes,\u201d I said. I stood up slowly, covering my body with the bottom duvet, and wrapping it around me as Gaba\u2019s brain was occupied with thoughts of how he had been duped, and might even be one of Sean Augustus Arnet\u2019s pastries. Yes, I enjoyed that one. \u201cBut the commissioner\u2026\u201d Gaba said. \u201cHe talked to him.\u201d \u201cHe talked to someone,\u201d I said.

ACTION: RACHAEL PULLS PETRA FROM TUBE ONTO METAL-DECK

SPEAKER: PETRA: "I DON'T KNOW"  
OBSERVE: PETRA SEES GABA IN ZODIAC RACING TOWARD HER

ROLE: GABA SITS ARMCHAIR END-OF-BED CLEANING MP5 LIBERATED-FROM-AMERICANS

REFLECTION: NAUGHTY WITH GABA > FANTASIES ABOUT SRU-LEADER

SPEAKER: GABA: "THREE GUYS BARRY CALLED PASTRIES"  
SPEAKER: PETRA: "PATSIES. FALL-GUYS. DUPES."

SPEAKER: PETRA: "IT WASN'T FAKE, THEN?"  
SPEAKER: GABA: "WHO KNOWS ∧ WHO CARES? WE'RE DONE WITH THIS ONE, CONSTABLE"  
SPEAKER: GABA: "BARRY WAS LAST-MINUTE REPLACEMENT; ORIGINAL LEADER SICK; BARRY HAPPENED TO BE IN GREENLAND"  
SPEAKER: PETRA: "WHAT WAS BARRY'S LAST NAME?"  
SPEAKER: GABA: "CAN'T REMEMBER; BROWN HAIR, BORING CLOTHES, BLAND ACCENT—NOTHING MEMORABLE"  
SPEAKER: PETRA: "HIS NAME IS BARRY AUGUST"

SPEAKER: PETRA: "AND WHAT ABOUT SAARULLIK ∧ TAATERAAQ?"  
SPEAKER: GABA: "EN ROUTE TO NUUK; NEXT FLIGHT; YOU CAN TAKE SHIP UP COAST"

SPEAKER: PETRA: "SEAN AUGUSTUS ARNET, LAST-MINUTE REPLACEMENT WHO CONFISCATED FAKE DIAMONDS"  
SPEAKER: PETRA: "DIAMONDS WE THINK ARE FAKE ∧ REAL-DEAL MEN WITH GUNS PROBABLY MADE HEFTY BONUS IN GREENLAND"

SPEAKER: PETRA: "HE TALKED TO SOMEONE"
